root_text.sn23.7:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ rādhaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn23.7:1.6: Yato kho, rādha, ariyasāvako imesaṁ pañcannaṁ upādānakkhandhānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti—
root_text.sn23.7:1.7: ayaṁ vuccati, rādha, ariyasāvako sotāpanno avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyano”ti.

translation_text.sn23.7:1.2: When Venerable Rādha was seated to one side, the Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn23.7:1.3: “Rādha, there are these five grasping aggregates.
translation_text.sn23.7:1.5: The grasping aggregates of form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn23.7:1.6: When a noble disciple truly understands these five grasping aggregates’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape,
translation_text.sn23.7:1.7: they’re called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
